close_reason: Added detect_macfuse_backend() in fuse/platform.rs that distinguishes FSKit (active/registered), kext (loaded), NotActivated, NotInstalled. FuseTBackend::mount now pre-checks before fuse_mount and returns FuseUnavailable with multi-line activation guidance instead of letting MFMount.framework block on a System Settings GUI prompt. Verified empirically: prior build hung indefinitely; new build exits in <1s with actionable instructions for both FSKit (preferred) and kext (legacy) activation paths. 6 unit tests cover the parsers (systemextensionsctl + kmutil) and the user-facing label/instructions.
description: macFUSE's FSKit extensions (io.macfuse.app.fsmodule.macfuse.appex, io.macfuse.app.fsmodule.macfuse-local.appex) exist at /Library/Filesystems/macfuse.fs/Contents/Resources/macfuse.app/Contents/Extensions/ but aren't registered as system extensions yet. Determine: does first mount trigger activation? Does user need to enable in System Settings > Extensions? Should turnkey-composed detect and guide the user? Add runtime detection of FSKit vs kext backend.
title: Handle macFUSE FSKit extension activation
